statement: step-loop compiles agent specs without cwd; loop-step agents run context-blind

evidence.quote: `pi-workflows/src/step-loop.ts:187` ‚Äî `compileAgentSpec(agentSpec, resolvedInput, options.templateEnv)` ‚Äî 3 args, no `cwd`. Compare the sibling `step-agent.ts:133` which passes `ctx.cwd` (4th arg). `step-shared.ts:147` documents contextBlocks inject only 'if ‚Ä¶ a .project/ directory exists at cwd.' Omitting cwd silently skips injection. The fix landed in step-agent but not step-loop.
.context absence: no `.context` item names contextBlocks-in-loop or compileAgentSpec.

action: Pass `cwd` (the loop `ctx.cwd`) as the 4th arg to `compileAgentSpec` in `step-loop.ts:187`, mirroring the sibling fix already in `step-agent.ts:133`, so contextBlocks injection is not silently skipped for loop-dispatched agents.
verify: Confirm `step-loop.ts:187` still passes 3 args; add a loop-step regression that asserts contextBlocks reach the agent when a `.project/` dir exists at cwd.

statement: resolvePromptField misclassifies single-line inline prompts containing "/" as template paths

evidence.quote: `pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts:25` ‚Äî `if (value.endsWith(".md") || value.endsWith(".txt") || (value.includes("/") && !value.includes("\n")))` treats a single-line inline prompt containing `/` as a template path. Only a newline-guard was added; the `/`-in-single-line case is still misclassified.
.context absence: `resolvePromptField` ‚Üí NONE.

action: Tighten the heuristic at `agent-spec.ts:25` so a single-line inline prompt containing `/` is not treated as a template file path (the newline-guard alone is insufficient); disambiguate inline vs file-path prompts explicitly.
verify: Confirm the `value.includes("/") && !value.includes("\n")` branch remains; add a case where a one-line inline prompt with a slash dispatches as inline text, not a missing template file.

statement: parseModelSpec pins bare (slash-less) model ids to provider="anthropic"

evidence.quote: `pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1181` ‚Äî `return { provider: "anthropic", modelId: spec };` for any slash-less model id.
.context absence: topic search `provider`+`anthropic` and `parseModelSpec` ‚Üí NONE; 0 behavior-monitor items in `.context`.

action: Stop defaulting slash-less model ids to `anthropic` in `parseModelSpec`; require an explicit provider or resolve it from a model registry so a bare non-Anthropic id is not silently misrouted.
verify: Confirm the `return { provider: "anthropic", modelId: spec }` default at :1181; add a test that a bare non-Anthropic model id is either rejected or routed to its correct provider.

statement: output.format:text combined with tools[] produces prose-only output with no compile-time conflict check

evidence.quote: no conflict check in `agent-spec.ts`/`compile.ts` (grep `format`+`tool`/`conflict` ‚Üí none). (Partially mooted by re-established FGAP-124: in-process jit can't execute tools at all.)
.context absence: `output.format` ‚Üí NONE.

action: Add a compile-time conflict check (in `agent-spec.ts`/`compile.ts`) that rejects or warns when `output.format: text` is combined with a non-empty `tools[]`. Note the report flags this as partially mooted by re-established FGAP-124 (in-process jit can't execute tools at all) ‚Äî reconcile scope before fixing.
verify: Re-grep `format`+`tool`/`conflict` in `agent-spec.ts`/`compile.ts`; first confirm current FGAP-124 (re-established) tool-execution status to decide whether this is still a distinct live gap.

statement: nested "workflow" step type is declared in spec but rejected at parse time

evidence.quote: `pi-workflows/src/workflow-spec.ts:292` ‚Äî `throw new WorkflowSpecError(filePath, "step '‚Ä¶': nested workflows ('workflow') are not yet supported")`.
.context absence: 'nested'+'workflow' only hits `.context` FGAP-008 (unrelated monitor-spec block).

action: Implement (or explicitly de-scope) the nested `workflow` step type so workflow-of-workflows composition is expressible; currently the parser throws.
verify: Confirm the throw at :292; implement nested-workflow expansion or document the de-scope decision.

evidence.quote: `pi-workflows/src/types.ts:56` `LoopSpec` has only `maxAttempts`, `attempts`, `steps`, `onExhausted` ‚Äî no `until`. `step-loop.ts:77` loops `for (‚Ä¶; iteration < maxAttempts; ‚Ä¶)`; termination is count + gate-break only.
.context absence: 'loop'+'until' hits are false positives (FGAP-119/122).

action: Add expression-based loop termination predicates (`until` / `untilBudgetRemaining`) to `LoopSpec` and honor them in the `step-loop` runtime, replacing count+gate-break-only termination.
verify: Confirm `LoopSpec` at types.ts:56 has no `until` field; add predicate evaluation to the loop and a loop-until-dry test.

statement: no workflow-level aggregate token budget (budget.maxTokens / spent() / remaining())

evidence.quote: the only `budget` in `pi-workflows` is the per-render `enforce-budget` tool / `x-prompt-budget` text truncation (`index.ts:883`), not a workflow-wide token accumulator; `workflow-spec.ts`/`state.ts` carry no budget field.
.context absence: `.context` FGAP-139 'budget' is `x-rhetorical-criteria` string budgets ‚Äî different concept.

action: Add a WorkflowSpec-level token-budget surface (accumulator with `maxTokens`/`spent()`/`remaining()`) enabling budget-aware termination / fail-on-overspend; distinct from the per-render `enforce-budget` truncation.
verify: Confirm `workflow-spec.ts`/`state.ts` carry no budget field; ensure the new surface is not conflated with `x-prompt-budget` truncation.

statement: parallel agent steps share one working tree; no worktree isolation option

evidence.quote: `pi-workflows/src/step-parallel.ts:84,141` ‚Äî `Promise.allSettled(promises)` fans out with a single shared `ctx.cwd`; grep for `worktree`/`isolat` across `pi-workflows/src` ‚Üí none.
.context absence: `worktree` ‚Üí NONE.

action: Add per-step worktree isolation for parallel/concurrent file-writing steps so `step-parallel` fan-out does not race on a single shared `ctx.cwd`.
verify: Confirm shared `ctx.cwd` at step-parallel.ts:84,141 and re-grep `worktree`/`isolat` absent; add isolation as a step opt.
